activities_projects.title: Research on Social Reformers
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the contributions of major social reformers in improving women's rights and caste equality
steps: Select one reformer from the chapter such as Raja Rammohan Roy, Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar, or Jyotirao Phule
Research their life and major reform activities
Prepare a chart showing their reforms and impact on society
Present your findings to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research skills and understand the importance of reformers in social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Widow Remarriage
activities_projects.objective: Understand the social challenges faced by widows and the reforms introduced to improve their lives
steps: Read the section about widow remarriage in the chapter
Identify problems faced by widows in the 19th century
Explain the role of reformers who supported widow remarriage
Write a short case study describing the social reform
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of social reform laws
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis and explanation in the case study (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate on Women’s Education
activities_projects.objective: Examine different viewpoints on women's education in the 19th century
steps: Divide students into two groups
One group presents arguments supporting women's education
The other group presents challenges faced in promoting women's education in the past
Conduct a structured classroom debate
Summarize the conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how education played a role in social transformation

activities_projects.title: Analysis of Caste Reform Movements
activities_projects.objective: Understand how caste reform movements challenged social inequality
steps: Read about caste discrimination mentioned in the chapter
List reform movements that opposed caste discrimination
Create a chart showing problems and reform solutions
Discuss how these reforms influenced society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the efforts made to reduce caste-based discrimination

activities_projects.title: Role of Print Culture in Social Reform
activities_projects.objective: Explore how books, newspapers, and magazines helped spread reform ideas
steps: Read the section on print culture in the chapter
Identify examples where newspapers discussed social issues
Write a short report on how print media influenced reform movements
Share your report with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of communication in social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of report and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparison Chart: Past and Present Status of Women
activities_projects.objective: Compare the position of women in the 19th century with the present day
steps: List the social restrictions faced by women in the past
Identify opportunities available for women today
Create a comparison chart showing differences
Explain the reasons for these changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking about social progress
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and completeness of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Equality
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of equality and social justice
steps: Form groups of 4–5 students
Discuss the meaning of equality in society
Identify examples of discrimination mentioned in the chapter
Suggest ways to promote equality today
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness about social justice and equality
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and relevance of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Short Documentary Presentation
activities_projects.objective: Understand reform movements through visual learning
steps: Search for a short documentary about social reform movements in India
Watch the documentary carefully
Note important points related to women and caste reform
Present a summary to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain a deeper understanding of reform movements through multimedia sources
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Essay Writing on Social Reform Movements
activities_projects.objective: Develop writing skills and understanding of historical reforms
steps: Review the chapter content
Write an essay on the topic 'Role of Social Reformers in Modern India'
Include examples of important reforms and their impact
Submit the essay for evaluation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how reform movements shaped Indian society

activities_projects.title: Map Activity: Centers of Reform Movements
activities_projects.objective: Identify important regions associated with social reform movements
steps: Identify places associated with major reformers such as Bengal and Maharashtra
Mark these locations on the map of India
Write the name of the reformer associated with each place
Explain the significance of these regions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the geographical spread of reform movements
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct map marking and explanation (10 marks)
